name: Pau Casals
abstract: Pablo Casals (29 December 1876 – 22 October 1973), also known by his birth name, Pau Casals i Defilló (Catalan: [ˈpaw kəˈzalz i ðəfiˈʎo]), was a Catalan cellist, composer, and conductor, born in Spain. He made many recordings throughout his career of solo, chamber, and orchestral music, including some as conductor, but he is perhaps best…

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: Pablo Casals, who I think he recorded some Bach suites in his 90’s and he said he was practicing and he finally learned how to play them right

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got some very rare recordings of Pablo Casals. I’ve always liked the way he played Bach and Mozart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Gold
about_quotes.evidence: He was also Pablo Casals' first call in the Bach orchestra suites at Marlboro.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Gold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I was thinkin' about Pablo Casals, not Pablo Picasso, he wanted to do some unaccompanied things like Pablo Casals

about_quotes.speaker: Putter Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I read this interview of Pablo Casals and he was talking about how fretless instruments
about_quotes.doc_title: Putter Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 27, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: the development of the cello had really stopped right about the time of Pablo Casals.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
